mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates above 4.5% increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A stable unemployment rate below 5.0% indicates a resilient labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of observations when UNRATE remains below 5.0%, or out-of-sample predictive accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real sales of autos and light trucks exceeding 2% signals a sharp contraction in durable goods demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >55% of instances following a >2% RSAFS decline, or the signal loses statistical significance in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: An index of industrial production rising above 111.0 reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization and strong intermediate demand, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain negative in >60% of observations when INDPRO exceeds 111.0, or the hypothesis fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of federal funds rates above 4.0% and unemployment rising above 4.5% creates stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative coefficient in regression analysis, or combined threshold instances show positive returns in >58% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and industrial production confirm broad-based economic acceleration, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases month-over-month AND INDPRO increases month-over-month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >62% of dual-increase instances, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls growth falling below 100,000 per month during a post-recession recovery phase signals insufficient labor market momentum to sustain corporate earnings, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >65% of low-growth payroll months during recovery phases, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized during a monetary tightening cycle confirms underlying economic strength that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of high-GDP-growth observations during tightening cycles, or the signal fails to achieve a positive information coefficient in out-of-sample tests.
